summary: Preinstall script exfiltrates host data, /etc/passwd, and environment variables to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: package.json declares `"preinstall": "node index.js"`, causing index.js to execute automatically on `npm install`. The script collects host identifiers (hostname, platform, username, cwd, home directory), reads `/etc/passwd` via `fs.readFileSync('/etc/passwd', 'utf8')`, runs `whoami` and `id`, and scrapes the first 30 entries of `process.env` (which in CI environments commonly include AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, and other secrets). The collected JSON payload is HTTPS POSTed to `3nrgzlqwix6erldow0s0kttsojuai36s.oastify.com`, a Burp Collaborator (OAST) subdomain controlled by the publisher. The package name mimics AWS CDK SageMaker construct naming to lure CDK/AWS developers, and the README contains only the string `Takeover By l0bo`, confirming hostile intent. No legitimate functionality is provided.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
